description: Admin Monitoring Dashboard (Fintech) — Live + 24h + 7d ops dashboard at admin.instnews.net/monitoring covering per-source ingestion latency, AI pipeline health, upstream API headroom (X rate-limit, Polygon, EDGAR, Stripe), and authoritative AWS + X API cost tracking. EMF-only metric emission (no new infra), uPlot charts, admin-only behind existing VPN + internal ALB. 13 stories: 1 metrics helper, 1 IAM/CDK update, 3 producer instrumentations, 2 admin APIs, 1 dashboard scaffold, 4 panels, 1 anomaly-badge polish. Source PRD: tasks/prd-admin-monitoring-dashboard.md

userStories.title: EMF metrics helper (app/services/metrics.py)
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I want a single Python helper that emits a CloudWatch EMF log line so producers across the app can publish metrics without duplicating the EMF JSON schema.
acceptanceCriteria: app/services/metrics.py defines emit_metric(namespace, metric_name, value, unit='Count', dimensions=None, **extra_fields) that prints one valid EMF JSON line to stdout
EMF payload conforms to spec: top-level _aws.Timestamp (ms), _aws.CloudWatchMetrics with Namespace/Dimensions/Metrics[{Name,Unit}], dimension values at top level, extra_fields merged
emit_metrics(namespace, metrics=[{name,value,unit}], dimensions, **extra) variant for multi-metric single-line emission
Context manager timed(namespace, metric_name, dimensions=...) measures wall time and emits on exit
tests/services/test_metrics.py validates JSON shape, dimension handling, and the context manager
mypy app/services/metrics.py exits 0
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Foundation for all later producer stories. Keep log volume low via multi-metric variant.
test_assertions: python3 -c "from app.services.metrics import emit_metric; emit_metric('SIG', 'FeedLatency', 1.5, 'Seconds', {'Source':'SeekingAlpha'})" exits 0 and prints valid JSON

python3 -c "import json, contextlib; from io import StringIO; from app.services.metrics import emit_metric; buf=StringIO()\nwith contextlib.redirect_stdout(buf): emit_metric('X','Y',1.0,'Count',{'A':'b'})\nd=json.loads(buf.getvalue().strip()); assert '_aws' in d and d['A']=='b' and d['Y']==1.0" exits 0
pytest tests/services/test_metrics.py -v exits 0
mypy app/services/metrics.py exits 0
userStories.implementation_notes: Implemented app/services/metrics.py (stdlib-only) exposing emit_metric(namespace, metric_name, value, unit='Count', dimensions=None, **extra), emit_metrics(namespace, metrics=[{name,value,unit}], dimensions=None, **extra), and @contextmanager timed(namespace, metric_name, dimensions=None, unit='Milliseconds', **extra). Each helper prints one compact EMF JSON line to stdout using print(..., flush=True) so the ECS awslogs driver auto-parses into CloudWatch metrics with zero SDK calls. Design notes: (1) Dimensions outer array always has length 1 per EMF schema (minItems:1); when no dimensions are passed, the inner DimensionSet is [] which EMF permits (minItems:0) — so empty emissions render as 'Dimensions': [[]]. (2) Dimension VALUES are always coerced to strings via str() because CloudWatch rejects non-string dimension targets; ints/floats/bools all work. (3) Timestamp is int(time.time()*1000), sampled at emit time (not function entry). (4) timed() wraps in try/finally so the metric is emitted even when the block raises, and the exception propagates. (5) timed(unit='Seconds') divides the elapsed ms by 1000 before emission; unit='Microseconds' multiplies by 1000; any other unit emits raw ms. (6) emit_metrics() with an empty metrics list raises ValueError (CloudWatch rejects empty Metrics arrays). Test file tests/services/test_metrics.py — 10 tests, all pytest + capfd-based, covering: basic shape + timestamp range, multi-metric variant, empty-list rejection, timed elapsed >= 45ms for a 50ms sleep, timed emits on exception, timed Seconds unit conversion, dimensions=None and dimensions={} both render [[]], non-string dimension coercion (int/float/bool), and single-compact-line invariant. Self-validation: pytest passed 10/10; mypy --follow-imports=silent exit 0; full regression 236 passed 1 skipped. Commit b326fb4. EMF spec fetched live from docs.aws.amazon.com — confirmed Dimensions is list-of-lists and Timestamp is ms-since-epoch int.
userStories.test_evidence: All artifacts at jojo/test_results/US-001/attempt-1/. Assertion 1 stdout (assertion1_smoke.log): one-line EMF JSON with _aws.CloudWatchMetrics[0]={Namespace:'SIG', Dimensions:[['Source']], Metrics:[{Name:'FeedLatency',Unit:'Seconds'}]}, top-level Source='SeekingAlpha', FeedLatency=1.5, exit=0. Assertion 2 (assertion2_heredoc.log): heredoc variant printed 'ok', exit=0. Assertion 3 (assertion3_pytest.log): pytest tests/services/test_metrics.py -v → 10 passed in 0.15s, exit=0. Assertion 4 (assertion4_mypy.log): 'Success: no issues found in 1 source file', exit=0. Regression (regression.log): 236 passed, 1 skipped, 1 warning in 6.56s, exit=0 — matches implementor's reported baseline exactly. Sanity (sanity_shape.log): Timestamp 1776462732790 is int-ms within 60s of now, Dimensions=[['Source']] (list-of-lists), Metrics=[{Name:'FeedLatency',Unit:'Seconds'}], top-level Source + FeedLatency present → SHAPE_OK. Sanity (sanity_roundtrip.log): emit_metrics_multi OK, timed_elapsed OK (Dur=50.06ms), timed_exception_safe OK (exception propagates, metric still emitted), empty_dims_shape OK ([[]] rendered for dimensions=None).
userStories.test_report: US-001 PASS. All 4 prd-defined assertions pass (smoke one-liner, heredoc structural check, pytest 10/10, mypy scoped to metrics.py). Regression baseline 236 passed + 1 skipped reproduced — no new failures. Sanity checks confirm EMF wire shape matches the spec: _aws.Timestamp is an integer ms-since-epoch within 60s of current time; _aws.CloudWatchMetrics[0] has Namespace/Dimensions[[...]]/Metrics[{Name,Unit}] exactly; dimension values and metric values live at the top level as EMF targets. Exception-safe behavior of timed() confirmed — RuntimeError propagates and the metric line is still emitted. Multi-metric emit_metrics() and empty-dimensions fallback to Dimensions=[[]] both behave as specified. Artifact dir: jojo/test_results/US-001/attempt-1/ (validate.sh + per-assertion logs + summary.log). No flags.

userStories.title: IAM + CDK — CloudWatch/CostExplorer read for admin task
userStories.description: As an SRE, I want the admin ECS task to have IAM permission to read CloudWatch metrics and Cost Explorer data, so the dashboard can call GetMetricData and GetCostAndUsage from the container.
acceptanceCriteria: infra/stack.py adds an IAM policy to the admin task role granting cloudwatch:GetMetricData, cloudwatch:ListMetrics, ce:GetCostAndUsage, ce:GetDimensionValues on Resource:*
cdk diff shows only the IAM policy diff (no other resource churn)
Deploy applied via cdk deploy
A one-shot script running in the admin ECS task can call boto3 cloudwatch.list_metrics and ce.get_cost_and_usage without AccessDenied
cdk synth runs cleanly
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: CE has no resource-level ARN support; scope=* is required. Cost Explorer calls cost $0.01/request — cache aggressively in US-007.
test_assertions: cd infra && npx cdk diff 2>&1 | grep -E 'IAM|PolicyDocument' matches expected additions
Inside admin ECS task: python3 -c "import boto3; boto3.client('cloudwatch').list_metrics(MaxResults=1)" exits 0
Inside admin ECS task: python3 -c "import boto3; boto3.client('ce').get_cost_and_usage(TimePeriod={'Start':'2026-04-10','End':'2026-04-17'}, Granularity='DAILY', Metrics=['UnblendedCost'])" exits 0
cd infra && npx cdk synth > /dev/null exits 0
userStories.implementation_notes: Added `aws_iam as iam` to the cdk imports in `infra/stack.py` and attached a single inline `iam.PolicyStatement` to `admin_task.task_role` (the runtime role that boto3 calls from the admin container assume). Statement: Effect=Allow, Actions=[cloudwatch:GetMetricData, cloudwatch:ListMetrics, ce:GetCostAndUsage, ce:GetDimensionValues], Resource=['*']. CE does not support resource-level ARNs so wildcard is required; CloudWatch GetMetricData/ListMetrics also intentionally wildcarded to match the namespaces the dashboard will query (InstantNews/Ingestion, InstantNews/AIPipeline, InstantNews/Twitter, etc.) — narrowing is not useful here because resource-ARN scoping for GetMetricData isn't granular enough to gate per-namespace anyway. Self-validation: (1) `cd infra && npx cdk synth > /tmp/us002_synth.log 2>&1` → exit 0. (2) `cd infra && npx cdk diff` → exit 0 and diff is exactly one IAM Statement addition (AdminTask/TaskRole/DefaultPolicy) on Resource=* with the four listed actions, plus the automatic DependsOn wiring CDK adds to resources that reference the admin task role (AdminService/Service, its SecurityGroup, and its SecurityGroupIngress all gain `AdminTaskTaskRoleDefaultPolicy80ADD05C` alongside the pre-existing `AdminTaskTaskRole229B8B1F`). That DependsOn churn is cosmetic — no resource is replaced, no physical ID changes. Full diff captured at /tmp/us002_diff.log. **DEPLOY DEFERRED**: `cdk deploy` was NOT run from this agent — team lead batches all stack deploys at the end of the dashboard PRD. Post-deploy assertion (boto3 calls from admin task) can only run after that deploy lands; tester should treat that assertion as deferred and validate synth/diff shape only.

userStories.title: Instrument per-source pollers with EMF metrics
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I want every source_poller tick to emit EMF metrics (latency, item count, fetch status) so the dashboard can render per-source health.
acceptanceCriteria: app/services/source_poller.py _run_once emits one EMF line per tick with NewItems, IngestLatencySeconds (median fetched_at-published over new rows), FetchDurationMs dimensioned by {Source, SourceType}
On fetch exception emits FetchErrors=1 with dimension ErrorType
Namespace InstantNews/Ingestion
Per-source intervals unchanged from prior stories
aws cloudwatch list-metrics --namespace InstantNews/Ingestion shows >= 3 metric names across >= 3 source dimensions after soak
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001. EMF is log-driven — no extra CloudWatch cost beyond existing log ingestion.
test_assertions: Unit test mocks fetch_feed with 2 items + known published timestamps; runs _run_once; captures stdout; asserts exactly one EMF JSON line with NewItems=2 and IngestLatencySeconds within range

Post-deploy: aws cloudwatch get-metric-statistics --namespace InstantNews/Ingestion --metric-name NewItems --dimensions Name=Source,Value=SeekingAlpha --start-time $(date -u -d '-10 min' +%FT%TZ) --end-time $(date -u +%FT%TZ) --period 60 --statistics Sum returns >= 1 datapoint
mypy app/services/source_poller.py exits 0

userStories.title: Instrument AI pipeline with EMF metrics
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I want the AI pipeline to emit queue depth, batch duration, and backend-choice counters so the dashboard shows when MiniMax is rate-limited and falling back to Claude.
acceptanceCriteria: app/services/ai_pipeline.py emits QueueDepth gauge once per dispatcher loop iteration
app/services/bedrock_analysis.py emits per-article BackendChosen counter with dimension {Backend: minimax|claude|bedrock} after each _call_model
Emits BatchSize and BatchDurationMs on each analyze_articles_batch completion
Namespace InstantNews/AIPipeline
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001. Watch for emission overhead on high-throughput path — keep it to one log line per batch.
test_assertions: Unit test mocks Bedrock clients to simulate MiniMax failure -> Claude success; captures stdout; asserts EMF line has BackendChosen with Backend=claude
pytest tests/services/test_ai_pipeline_metrics.py -v exits 0
Post-deploy: aws cloudwatch get-metric-statistics --namespace InstantNews/AIPipeline --metric-name QueueDepth --start-time ... --statistics Maximum returns datapoints
mypy app/services/ai_pipeline.py app/services/bedrock_analysis.py exits 0

userStories.title: Instrument X API calls with rate-limit + cost metrics
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I want every /2/tweets/search/recent response to emit rate-limit headroom + tweet/user counts so the dashboard shows X API health and live-estimated monthly spend.
acceptanceCriteria: app/services/twitter_source.py TwitterClient._get on 200 emits EMF metrics: RateLimitRemaining (from x-rate-limit-remaining header), RateLimitLimit, TweetsBilled (len response.data), UsersBilled (len includes.users), dimension {Endpoint: search_recent}
On 429 emits RateLimited=1 with same dimension
Namespace InstantNews/Twitter
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001. Don't disturb existing since_id cost-saving machinery.
test_assertions: Unit test mocks requests.Session.get returning 200 + headers + body; calls _get; asserts EMF line shape includes RateLimitRemaining and TweetsBilled values
Unit test mocks 429; asserts RateLimited=1 emitted
pytest tests/services/test_twitter_metrics.py -v exits 0

Post-deploy: aws cloudwatch get-metric-statistics --namespace InstantNews/Twitter --metric-name TweetsBilled --statistics Sum --start-time $(date -u -d '-1h' +%FT%TZ) --end-time $(date -u +%FT%TZ) --period 300 returns datapoints matching observed call volume
mypy app/services/twitter_source.py exits 0

userStories.title: Admin API POST /admin/api/metrics/cloudwatch
userStories.description: As a frontend engineer, I want one admin endpoint that proxies cloudwatch:GetMetricData so the dashboard can fetch multi-metric time series in one call.
acceptanceCriteria: New blueprint app/admin/metrics.py exposes POST /admin/api/metrics/cloudwatch (admin role check)
Request JSON: {range: '1h'|'24h'|'7d', queries: [{id, namespace, metric, dimensions, stat}]}
Resolves range to start_time, end_time, period (1h->60s, 24h->60s, 7d->3600s)
Calls cloudwatch:GetMetricData with up to 500 queries per request
Response JSON: {series: {[id]: {timestamps, values}}}
Server-side in-memory cache (60s TTL) keyed by query hash
Unauthenticated request returns 401; non-admin returns 403
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-002 for IAM. Use POST because GetMetricData payload can be large.
test_assertions: curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/admin/api/metrics/cloudwatch -H 'Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_JWT' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"range":"1h","queries":[{"id":"q1","namespace":"InstantNews/Ingestion","metric":"NewItems","dimensions":{"Source":"SeekingAlpha"},"stat":"Sum"}]}' returns 200 with series.q1.timestamps array
curl without Authorization header returns 401
curl with non-admin JWT returns 403
Second identical call within 60s returns identical body (cache hit)
mypy app/admin/metrics.py exits 0

userStories.title: Admin API GET /admin/api/metrics/cost
userStories.description: As a frontend engineer, I want the dashboard to fetch authoritative cost figures (AWS Cost Explorer daily totals + X API usage) without the frontend holding IAM credentials.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /admin/api/metrics/cost?range=7d returns {aws:{by_service,daily_totals}, x_api:{used_this_month, quota, reset_at}}
AWS: ce.get_cost_and_usage(Granularity='DAILY', GroupBy=[{Type:'DIMENSION',Key:'SERVICE'}], Metrics=['UnblendedCost'])

X API: implementor MUST WebFetch current X docs to verify /2/usage/tweets availability on Basic tier; if unavailable, fall back to summing InstantNews/Twitter.TweetsBilled for the month * $0.005 with estimated=true flag in response
Cache: 1h TTL on Cost Explorer (response-level), 5 min on X usage
Admin role check; non-admin returns 403
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: OQ-1 resolution lives here. Cost Explorer = $0.01/request, cache discipline is mandatory.
test_assertions: curl -H 'Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_JWT' 'http://localhost:8000/admin/api/metrics/cost?range=7d' returns 200 with keys aws and x_api
Second identical call within 1h returns response with header X-Cache: HIT
curl with non-admin JWT returns 403
Response x_api section includes either {used_this_month, quota, reset_at} or {estimated: true, used_this_month}
mypy app/admin/metrics.py exits 0

Time-range selector with chips: 1h (Live), 24h, 7d; Live auto-refresh every 10s, 24h/7d every 60s
Shared fetchMetrics(queries, range) function POSTs to /admin/api/metrics/cloudwatch
uPlot imported as charting library
Empty panel placeholders with section headers: Ingestion, AI Pipeline, Upstream APIs, Cost
No JS console errors on load

acceptanceCriteria: panel--ingestion renders one tile per source (17 tiles: 15 RSS + Twitter + TruthSocial)

Tile shows: source name + rss|social badge, p50 latency (seconds) with green/yellow/red badge (thresholds <30s green, <120s yellow, >=120s red), p95 in small text, items/min sparkline (last 60 points)
Tiles sort by p95 desc (worst first)
Live mode: tiles recompute every 10s with smooth CSS value transitions
Click a tile to pin: right-side detail drawer opens with 24h latency uPlot line chart for that source
No JS console errors

userStories.title: Upstream APIs panel — X rate-limit meter + Polygon/EDGAR tiles
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to see live X API rate-limit headroom (450 cap) plus Polygon/EDGAR request-rate tiles so I know we're not about to be throttled.

Each tile links to the relevant CloudWatch Logs Insights saved query (new tab)

If x_api.estimated=true, show footnote 'estimated' under the monthly progress bar

userStories.title: Anomaly highlights — red/yellow badges + summary counter
userStories.description: As an admin, I want badges on source tiles and pipeline widgets that turn yellow/red when a metric crosses a threshold so I can see problems at a glance without alert fatigue.
acceptanceCriteria: Shared helper classifyStatus(value, warn, crit) returns 'ok'|'warn'|'crit'

THRESHOLDS defined centrally in frontend/src/admin-monitoring.js: {ingestion_p95_s:{warn:60,crit:180}, ai_queue_depth:{warn:100,crit:500}, x_rate_limit_pct:{warn:50,crit:80}, minimax_fallback_pct:{warn:20,crit:50}}
Badge classes badge--ok | badge--warn | badge--crit use CSS tokens --green / --yellow / --red
Summary counter at top of page: 'X criticals / Y warnings' that, when clicked, filters panels to show only critical/warning tiles
Typecheck passes
npm run build exits 0
Verify in browser
userStories.notes: No paging, visual-only. Thresholds are design-time constants; tune later via PR if noisy.

Playwright: click 'X criticals' counter; assert only critical tiles remain visible
